observe: You can bring things into being here, and end them too. Both take intention.
fault: Something finished and never left. It's still holding a seat it doesn't need.
repair: Spawn what you need. Terminate what's done. Watch the tree change shape.
escape: Clean up before you go — nothing lingers past its use here.

observe: Processes are created and terminated through explicit operations.
fault: A completed process has not been terminated.
repair: Issue the appropriate process creation or termination command.

hints: Creation and termination are both deliberate calls — nothing happens automatically.
